generated_oml:
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "1/10 scale vehicle LiDAR sensor transmitting point cloud via UDP"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle state sensor delivering metrics via ROS2"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateMetricsData
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    base:desc "LiDAR point cloud transmitted as series of point coordinates via UDP"
]

instance VehicleStateMetricsData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Vehicle state metrics transmitted over ROS2 messages"
]

instance SerializedLiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "LiDAR point cloud serialized and unit-aligned in data translation layer"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance UnitAlignedVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state metrics aligned and normalized by Data Translation component"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateMetricsData
]

instance ClusteredObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Point cloud partitioned into object tracks by Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking component"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance ACCDesiredSpeedCommand : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired speed command computed by ACC algorithm in computation offloading service"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCComputationEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData UnitAlignedVehicleState
]

instance VehicleMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle motor actuator controlled by digital twin"
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering actuator for vehicle steering"
]

instance ServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo actuator"
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation component serializes, aligns, and normalizes all streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance PointCloudClusteringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking component partitions cloud into object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ACCComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Computes ACC desired speeds based on lead-vehicle distances"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ReportSavingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Handles saving of monitoring reports"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Displays real-time vehicle state, projected driving paths, object tracks; allows viewing, saving, generating reports"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, DrivingPathInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, GenerateReportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs ACC algorithm in cloud, returns desired speed to vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:provides IssueACCCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, or trigger emergency braking remotely"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, TriggerEmergencyBrakeAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction
]

instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into real-time vehicle state metrics"
]

instance DrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into projected driving paths"
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into detected object tracks"
]

instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Allows generation of detailed monitoring reports"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance IssueACCCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issues desired speed command to vehicle for ACC"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables ACC on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance TriggerEmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers emergency braking on vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sets desired speed for ACC"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "High‑level control commands such as target speed and steering angle are published by the Digital Twin control logic on ROS 2 topics. The Actuation FE receives these publications, performs data translation, and translates the desired speed into a motor voltage command and the steering setpoint into a servo position command. These actuator‑level commands are conveyed to the vehicle’s motor controller and servo driver, which execute the physical actuation. The Data Translation FE ensures inbound commands are correctly typed and unit‑converted. The system does not specify explicit authentication, conflict‑resolution, or feedback confirmation mechanisms in the provided documentation."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system requires a shared notion of time across all vehicle devices to enable computation offloading. The design mandates clock synchronization better than 100 ms—specifically a 100 ms accuracy due to hardware limitations—to satisfy safety‑critical vehicular application standards. Temporal granularity for digital‑physical data exchanges is therefore bounded by this 100 ms synchronization window."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Architecture follows the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture; key software components identified in Table I are mapped to Functional Entities (FEs) and deployed as individual ROS 2 packages, each containing one or more ROS 2 nodes. The User Entity hosts a User Interface FE split into 3D Visualization and Control Panel. The Digital Twin Entity bundles data‑management, control‑mechanisms, and Data Storage (which lacks a natural FE). The Physical Twin Entity encompasses sensor/actuator interfaces, processing such as Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, and device communication. This entity‑based decomposition across separate hardware nodes enables fine‑grained resource management and system‑wide coordination, though it deviates from ISO 23247 by exposing a Physical Twin FE."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Digital Twin software components communicate internally using ROS2 middleware (DDS). Inter‑entity communication between the vehicle (physical twin) and the user interface (digital twin) is performed over a Wi‑Fi wireless network using UDP. The network topology includes an edge node inside the vehicle hosting the data translation, point‑cloud processing and visualization services, and a client node in the user’s cockpit. Bandwidth requirements are dictated by the high‑throughput LiDAR point‑cloud stream; latency must remain sub‑millisecond to maintain real‑time visualization. No explicit reliability, redundancy or security mechanisms are documented."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "ISO 23247 ‑ Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing (Part 2: Reference Architecture) compliance is established. Interoperability is facilitated by optional use of OPC Unified Architecture for device‑to‑digital communication. No further certification or compliance framework details are provided in the document."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system implements Adaptive Cruise Control following ISO 15622‑2018 performance requirements and test procedures, and its V2X communication latency adheres to the constraints defined in 3GPP TS 22.185. No additional cybersecurity or safety protocol details are provided in the source."
]
